compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
Plan-review v4 PASS (0 blocking, 17 advisory) â€” design gated. (v3 was INDETERMINATE only due to a verify-step token-cap truncation, filed as bug spy-luge-wool; v4's tightened 7-AC form verified cleanly.) Advisory triage â€” all valid, folding into implementation:
- [T9/#6] First deploy has no :prev image -> rollback skips gracefully if :prev absent (tag :prev only after a known-good run).
- [T9/#10] Prune dangling review-bot image layers after a successful build (docker image prune -f scoped).
- [T5b/#11] git fetch gets --timeout / a wrapping timeout so a hung fetch never holds the flock across timer fires.
- [#12] Concrete health check: receiver /health endpoint + a synthetic review probe (assert the bot casts on a canary) â€” spec'd in the script.
- [G1G2/#1,#2] make config-check must run in BOTH test.yml AND gerrit-verify.yaml (gerrit-verify mirrors the gates inline) so a malformed config fails the Verified gate.
- [T3/#5] CORRECTION: the review-bot is a DOCKER container, not a systemd service â€” so the crash-loop storm guard is docker-compose restart policy (on-failure w/ max retries) + the deploy loop's SHA-keyed backoff, NOT systemd StartLimit (which only applies to the autodeploy oneshot itself). Will adjust.
- [G1G2/#3,#14] materialize-g2p-config.sh refreshes the g2p bind-mount copy of replication.config; observability.sh gains the AUTODEPLOY_ERROR probe; name monitoring_autodeploy.tf.
- [E1/#15] project.config == refs/meta/config terminology unified.
- [T5e/#16] thresholds (2m cadence, 30s health timeout, backoff 60s/2/15m) as named constants at the top of autodeploy.sh.
Starting the build with the lowest-risk piece: the CI config-validation gate (make config-check).

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
Plan re-review after box-adaptation: VERDICT=PASS (0 blocking, 0 indeterminate, 20 advisory). Triage:

REMEDIATED IN PLAN: What-section + AC1 updated to 'review-bot ONLY; replication/g2p/refs-meta DETECT-ONLY in v1' (removed the stale auto-apply wording); Stability Â§3 health-check reworded to liveness (process up + /health 200) consistent with box-adaptation Â§3; ADR 0026 added (docs/adr/0026-autodeploy-on-box-timer.md) covering on-box-timer-vs-GH-OIDC, copy-based build context, v1 review-bot-only narrowing, back-out.

RESOLVED BY COMMIT (reviewer compared the plan to the STALE committed autodeploy.sh â€” old git-checkout version; the box-adapted code was uncommitted in the working tree): 'no rsync / still uses /opt/rebar/src / no MIRROR_DIR / auto-applies replication+g2p / install-autodeploy.sh missing / first-run doesn't seed from .deployed_ref' â€” ALL implemented in the working-tree rewrite (mirror clone + rsync --delete + HTTPS guard + detect-only config + corrected systemd paths + .deployed_ref seed) and install-autodeploy.sh created; they align once committed.

JUDGED INVALID / justified-kept:
- bash '**' claimed to need bash 5.0+: FALSE. Arithmetic '**' works since bash 3.x; the box runs bash 5.2.15 (verified via SSM: 60*(2**3)=480). No change.
- python3 fork in log/err (perf): kept deliberately â€” correct JSON escaping > a sed hack that can mis-escape; ~1440 short-lived forks/day on a 2-min timer is negligible.
- first-build-no-:prev health-fail edge: on the box review-bot:latest always exists (bot is running) so have_prev=1 in practice; documented, not a live risk.

VALIDATION EVIDENCE: live SSM --dry-run of the exact rsync exclude set on the box confirmed .env is preserved (0 delete matches) and only stray *.bak files would be removed; mirror clone bootstrapped, remote is https, main tip resolved.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
LIVE STAGED ROLLOUT COMPLETE â€” box i-00880b2c7f13527c5, continuous auto-deploy is LIVE. All ACs verified on the real box (evidence below):

INSTALL (timer disabled): install-autodeploy.sh bootstrapped the mirror clone (/var/lib/rebar/mirror, HTTPS), installed rebar-autodeploy.{service,timer}, timer DISABLED. Verified is-enabled=disabled/is-active=inactive.

MANUAL DRY-RUN (bootstrap deploy): ran autodeploy.sh from the mirror with the unit env; seeded deployed-sha to pre-S2 f85f47a57. Result: deployed-sha f85f47a57->463739cdb; review-bot image 0ee335c7->1480b860; bot NOW carries S2 merge-change code (assemble.py + review_bot/gerrit_client.py present in the container â€” S5 PREREQ SATISFIED, bot was pre-S2); gerrit container id UNCHANGED (ef56267f, blast radius held); .env byte-identical (sha 6d31347f, 600 owner preserved); config detect-only fired (AUTODEPLOY_ERROR config_manual â€” replication/g2p/refs-meta signalled, NOT applied); :prev rollback tag created; health {status:ok}. AC1/AC2/AC3 + box-adaptation ACs âœ“.

ENABLE + AUTONOMOUS TIMER DEPLOY: systemctl enable --now; rewound deployed-sha to pre-S2; the TIMER (not manual) fired at 19:52:51 and autonomously rebuilt+restarted review-bot (redeployed+healthy; deploy complete: reflects 463739cdb), then the 19:54:59 fire logged 'up to date; no-op' (idempotent). Timer recurring every 2min. AC1 (â‰¤5min, no manual action, idempotent) âœ“.

SELF-HEAL (AC4): injected a forced health-check failure (/etc/rebar/autodeploy.env HEALTH_URL unreachable) + rewound deployed-sha; the real systemd service ran: build succeeded -> up -> health check FAILED -> 'ROLLING BACK to :prev' -> deployed-sha NOT advanced (stayed pre-S2) -> backoff recorded (fail#1, 60s) -> bot /health:8000 stayed UP ('last-known-good stays live') -> gerrit UNCHANGED. Cleaned up + restored to steady state (deployed-sha=main, bot=S2 healthy, timer re-enabled, clean no-op confirmed). AC4 âœ“; AC5 backoff proven (fail#1=60s; SHA-keyed reset by construction; systemd StartLimit storm-guard in the unit).

BLAST RADIUS (AC3): gerrit container id ef56267f unchanged across ALL deploys (dry-run, autonomous, self-heal); no Gerrit restart; refs/meta/config never auto-applied (detect-only).

OBSERVABILITY (AC7): observability.sh Â§4d publishes rebar/host:deploy_errors from AUTODEPLOY_ERROR journal markers; monitoring_autodeploy.tf CloudWatch alarm 'rebar-autodeploy-errors' APPLIED live (terraform apply -target; threshold 0, 2x300s, wired to rebar-gerrit-alerts SNS). Seeded the deploy-errors offset so test markers don't false-spike.

CI CONFIG-GATE (AC6): make config-check wired in test.yml AND gerrit-verify.yaml on main (verified); passes on valid config.

BACK-OUT (AC7): systemctl disable --now rebar-autodeploy.timer (documented in unit + ADR 0026); manual path (compose-up.sh) unchanged.

Code landed via Gerrit change 231 (LLM-Review+1, Verified+1, MERGED, replicated to main=463739cdb). Plan re-gated PASS after box-adaptation.

compiled_state.description:
# Continuous auto-deploy: the running Gerrit environment reflects `main` without manual deploy or restart

## Why
Landing a change on `main` does NOT update the running environment: the review-bot container's code,
`replication.config`, and g2p config need a MANUAL deploy (SSM / setup-*.sh) + sometimes a restart.
This drifts + toils (S2's merge-review code landed but the running bot is pre-S2; the S3 replication
fix was hand-applied). The environment must AUTO-REFLECT `main` â€” without ever destabilising the
LIVE, FAIL-CLOSED gate (no LLM-Review vote â‡’ no submit; a bad deploy could freeze all submissions).

## What
- **Trigger (RESOLVED): on-box systemd timer** `rebar-autodeploy.timer` (2-min) â†’ `.service` oneshot
  running `infra/scripts/autodeploy.sh`. Chosen over a GitHub-Actionsâ†’SSM `deploy.yml` because it adds
  NO GitHubâ†’AWS trust surface (no OIDC/`ssm:SendCommand` grant that would make the GH runner a
  lateral-movement path); the box already reads the PUBLIC mirror read-only + has an instance role.
- **Loop:** `flock -n` guard; fetch `origin/main`; no-op when `== deployed-sha`
  (`/var/lib/rebar/deployed-sha`, initialised to current HEAD on first run WITHOUT deploying, written
  atomically only on success). Deploy ONLY the mirror `main` tip (Verified-by-construction: it only
  advances via Gerrit submit). Change-detect per component via `git diff --name-only
  deployed-sha..origin/main` âˆ© a path manifest (review-bot: src/rebar/**, Dockerfile.reviewbot,
  pyproject, docker-compose; replication: replication.config; g2p: ini + materialize script).
- **Apply only what changed (v1 = review-bot ONLY):** review-bot â†’ tag current image `:prev`, sync
  source (see Box-adaptation), `docker compose build && up -d review-bot`. **replication/g2p AND
  `refs/meta/config` are DETECT-ONLY in v1** â€” a change is signalled (`AUTODEPLOY_ERROR`), never
  auto-applied (their correct apply needs a live-site copy + SSM PAT re-fetch whose failure modes must
  not sit in the unattended path guarding a fail-closed gate; v2 follow-up). See Box-adaptation Â§3.

## Stability â€” live-pipeline safety (load-bearing)
1. **Bounded blast radius:** never touch the `gerrit` container (explicit `review-bot` service target
   + a post-deploy assert the gerrit container id is unchanged); never modify `refs/meta/config` (gate
   labels/rules/ACLs). No Gerrit restart â€” only the bot container restarts, only on a code change.
2. **In-flight reviews delayed, not lost:** a restart drops in-flight webhooks, but the existing
   reconciler-backfill re-reviews them; the post-deploy health check confirms the reconciler is live.
3. **Self-heal, never freeze the gate:** after `up -d`, an END-TO-END health check (30s timeout â€”
   liveness: process up + `/health` 200) gates success; on failure â†’ immediate
   rollback to `:prev` + `up -d`; deployed-sha NOT advanced. Max frozen window = the health timeout.
4. **Failure handling = capped exponential backoff, NOT hard-disable** (mature-OSS consensus: Flux
   retryInterval/backoff; Argo CD retry backoff duration/factor/maxDuration; k8s controller-runtime
   ItemExponentialFailureRateLimiter 5msâ†’1000s; systemd v254 RestartSteps/RestartMaxDelaySec):
   - rollback keeps last-known-good RUNNING; retry the deploy with backoff base 60s, factor 2, cap
     15m â€” never auto-disable the loop (a hard-disable strands the env stale until a human acts).
   - **SHA-keyed reset (Argo CD's rule):** persist last-attempted-SHA + failure count; a NEW
     `origin/main` tip RESETS the backoff to base (a fix-forward deploys promptly); a known-bad SHA is
     not re-attempted faster than the cap (no thrash) and is never permanently blacklisted.
   - **Outer storm guard:** systemd `StartLimitIntervalSec`/`StartLimitBurst` on the RUNNING bot
     service catches a crash-looping container (â†’ failed, needs `reset-failed`) â€” reserved for the
     pathological case, NOT the primary retry path.
5. **Config validated before it touches Gerrit:** parse-check replication/g2p config pre-apply; a
   malformed config is skipped (prior retained) + alarmed. (Belt-and-suspenders to AC below.)

## Defense-in-depth: a CI gate that rejects malformed config (so it never reaches `main`)
`make config-check` â€” parse-validate every infra config: `git config -f {replication,project}.config
--list`; python configparser on the g2p ini template; `docker compose config -q`; `bash -n` on
`infra/**/*.sh`. Wired into `test.yml` (â‡’ runs in `gerrit-verify` â‡’ the `Verified` gate), so a
malformed config FAILS CI and can NEVER land on `main` â€” the auto-deploy never encounters one at
runtime (the runtime pre-apply check in Stability Â§5 is the belt-and-suspenders for a direct box edit).

## Observability + back-out
Structured deploy log (fromâ†’to SHA + components) + `rebar/host:deploy_errors` (host-probe grep of an
`AUTODEPLOY_ERROR` marker, mirroring voter_errors) + a CloudWatch alarm w/ SNS action; the
backoff/failure state is alarmed. Back-out: `systemctl disable --now rebar-autodeploy.timer`; the
manual path (compose-up.sh / setup-*.sh) is unchanged.

## Scope
Deploy orchestrator + systemd units + stability guards + the CI config-check + observability/back-out
on the existing compose stack. Reuses the idempotent setup/materialise appliers. refs/meta/config
auto-apply deferred (detect + signal). No change to gate semantics (S1/S2) or the 88ab flow.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A post-gate `main` change auto-reflects on the box (no manual action) â‰¤ 5 min; latency
      live-measured. Idempotent: a no-change run is a no-op; a change re-applies ONLY the changed
      component(s) â€” verified for review-bot code; a replication.config / g2p / refs-meta change emits
      `AUTODEPLOY_ERROR` and is NOT auto-applied in v1 (detect-only)
- [ ] review-bot rebuilt/restarted ONLY on a source-set change (docs-only change â‡’ no rebuild); after
      a code change the running bot reflects it end-to-end (a merge change gets the S2 merge tag)
- [ ] Blast radius: the `gerrit` container is provably untouched; `refs/meta/config` never
      auto-modified; no Gerrit restart. project.config changes are detected + signalled, not attempted
- [ ] Self-heal: a deliberately-broken bot image FAILS the health check â‡’ rollback to `:prev` (gate
      restored), deployed-sha not advanced (proven live)
- [ ] Failure backoff: repeated failure uses capped exponential backoff (base 60sâ†’cap 15m), keeps
      last-known-good running, and a NEW `main` SHA resets the backoff (fix-forward deploys promptly);
      the loop never auto-disables; the systemd StartLimit storm-guard is in place
- [ ] CI config-gate: `make config-check` runs in `test.yml`/`gerrit-verify` and FAILS on a
      deliberately-malformed config (proven) â€” a malformed config cannot reach `main`
- [ ] flock serializes overlapping fires; deploy log + `deploy_errors` metric + alarm applied;
      back-out (disable timer) tested; manual path still works; live end-to-end auto-deploy verified

## Box-adaptation of the deploy mechanism (grounded in live box i-00880b2c7f13527c5)

Live SSM inspection changed two assumptions in v1â€“v4, so the mechanism is adapted and v1's
**auto-apply surface is narrowed to the review-bot container only** (config = detect+signal).
The safety guarantees (blast radius, self-heal, backoff, config-check-at-CI, flock) are unchanged.

### Observed box facts (SSM)
- `/opt/rebar` is a plain **copy** of the repo root â€” NOT a git checkout (no `.git`; uid 502; a
  `.deployed_ref` marker). It is the compose `build.context` (`../..`) for both services; review-bot
  bakes source at **build** time (no source bind-mount), so a rebuild picks up new code.
- Real compose working dir: `/opt/rebar/infra/compose` (services `gerrit`, `review-bot`).
- The ONLY runtime state under `/opt/rebar` is `infra/compose/.env` (SSM-sourced secrets, git-ignored,
  0600, regenerated at boot by `fetch-secrets.sh`). All Docker state lives OUTSIDE `/opt/rebar` â€”
  named volumes (`gerrit_git`/`gerrit_index`/â€¦) + bind mounts under `/var/gerrit/*` â€” so a source
  resync cannot touch it. systemd 252. review-bot health `http://localhost:8000/health` â†’ `{"status":"ok"}`.

### Adapted mechanism
1. **Mirror clone (self-bootstrapping).** autodeploy maintains its own **regular** git clone at
   `MIRROR_DIR=/var/lib/rebar/mirror` â€” `git clone https://github.com/navapbc/rebar.git` (public HTTPS,
   no auth; a *regular* clone, NOT `--mirror`/`--bare`, so `origin/main` resolves for the diff/rev-parse
   ops). autodeploy self-bootstraps it if absent and **aborts if the remote is not `https://`** (supply-
   chain guard). ALL git ops â€” fetch `--prune`, `rev-parse origin/main`, the
   `git diff --name-only deployed..target` component change-detection, checkout of the target â€” run there.
2. **Source sync = `rsync -a --delete $MIRROR_DIR/ /opt/rebar/`** with hard excludes protecting runtime
   state: `--exclude '/.git'`, `--exclude 'infra/compose/.env'`, `--exclude '/.deployed_ref'`, plus the
   dev/state set (`.venv .terraform .serena .claude .tickets-tracker`). `.env` + Docker volumes are never
   touched; ownership kept 502:502.
3. **Apply â€” review-bot ONLY (v1 narrowed).** On a review-bot source-set change
   (`src/rebar/** infra/compose/Dockerfile.reviewbot infra/compose/docker-compose.yml pyproject.toml`):
   tag current image `:prev` (guarded â€” skip if none, first-build has no rollback target), `docker compose
   build review-bot && up -d review-bot` in `/opt/rebar/infra/compose`, END-TO-END health-gate (liveness:
   process up + `/health` 200), rollback to `:prev` on failure, blast-radius assert (gerrit container id
   unchanged), `docker image prune -f`. **replication.config, g2p, and refs/meta/config are DETECT-ONLY in
   v1** â€” a change is signalled (`AUTODEPLOY_ERROR` marker) for a manual operator apply, never auto-applied.
   Rationale: their correct apply needs live-site copy (`/var/gerrit/site/etc/â€¦`) and an SSM PAT re-fetch
   (`materialize-g2p-config.sh`) whose failure modes should not sit in the unattended path guarding a
   fail-closed gate; they are rare and already hand-applied. Auto-apply of config is a documented v2 follow-up.
4. **State/backoff unchanged.** `deployed-sha`/backoff/first-run-adopt as in v1; first run seeds the marker
   from `/opt/rebar/.deployed_ref` if present (else the mirror tip), still WITHOUT deploying. A persistent
   **git-fetch failure is instrumented** (`AUTODEPLOY_ERROR fetch_failed`) so a stalled mirror alarms.

### systemd + installer + scope additions
- **systemd fix:** `ExecStart=/opt/rebar/infra/scripts/autodeploy.sh` (not `/opt/rebar/src/â€¦`),
  `DEPLOY_REPO=/opt/rebar`, add `MIRROR_DIR=/var/lib/rebar/mirror`.
- **`infra/scripts/install-autodeploy.sh`** (new; mirrors `install-observability.sh`): self-bootstraps the
  mirror clone, installs the `.service`+`.timer` unit files, `daemon-reload`. **Ships the timer DISABLED**
  (staged rollout: enable only after a manual dry-run passes).
- The health check is a **liveness** probe (process up + `/health` 200), not a deep readiness check â€” the
  AC wording is narrowed accordingly.

### Additional acceptance criteria (box-adaptation)
- [ ] autodeploy syncs source into the copy-based `/opt/rebar` via a self-bootstrapping regular mirror
      clone (HTTPS-enforced) + `rsync --delete` with excludes; a live resync provably leaves
      `infra/compose/.env` byte-identical and the `gerrit` container + Docker volumes untouched
- [ ] v1 auto-applies review-bot ONLY; a replication/g2p/`refs/meta/config` change is detected + signalled
      (`AUTODEPLOY_ERROR`), never auto-applied
- [ ] `install-autodeploy.sh` installs units with the **timer disabled**; a manual dry-run
      (`systemctl start rebar-autodeploy.service`) applies a real `main` advance end-to-end on the box
      (review-bot rebuilt from synced source, health OK), evidenced by the deploy log SHA transition

file_impact.reason: deploy orchestrator: flock, deployed-sha, change-detect, per-component apply, health-check+rollback, SHA-keyed exponential backoff
oneshot deploy unit + StartLimit storm-guard on the bot service
run make config-check (CI gate rejects malformed config)

compiled_state.title: Continuous auto-deploy: running Gerrit environment reflects main without manual deploy or restart
